mnemonics.title: Fossil Fuel Types
mnemonics.content: C-P-N: Coal (solid), Petroleum (liquid), Natural gas (gaseous) - remember 'Coal, Pump, Natural' or think of them as solid rock, flowing liquid, and natural gas for cooking.

mnemonics.title: Coal Rank Order
mnemonics.content: PEAT-LBA: Peat → Lignite → Bituminous → Anthracite - Remember 'People Eat Apples To Live Better' or 'Please Let Big Animals' (Peat, Lignite, Bituminous, Anthracite)

mnemonics.title: Thermal Power Plant Components
mnemonics.content: B-T-G-C: Boiler → Turbine → Generator → Condenser - Think 'Boilers Turn Great Currents' or 'Big Turbines Generate Current'.

mnemonics.title: Environmental Impacts
mnemonics.content: GAP-W: G-Global warming, A-Acid rain, P-Pollution, W-Water contamination - Remember 'Greenhouse Gases Attack People's Welfare'.

mnemonics.title: Carnot Efficiency Formula
mnemonics.content: η = 1 - Tc/Th: Remember 'Not The Cold (Tc) But The Hot (Th) matters' - efficiency increases with higher temperature difference.

acronyms.usage: Simultaneous generation of electricity and heat from single fuel source
Technology to remove SO2 from flue gases in power plants
Gases like CO2, CH4 that trap heat in atmosphere causing global warming
Unit of heat energy; 1 BTU = 1055 joules
Mixture of propane and butane used as cooking fuel
Device that converts fuel gas to mechanical energy

visualTips.visualization: Imagine layers of dead plants and sea organisms (plankton) buried under sand and mud at the seabed for millions of years. Pressure and heat slowly squeeze out liquids and gases, leaving carbon-rich solids (coal) and liquids (petroleum). Draw a timeline from 300 million years ago to present.

Draw a circular loop: Boiler (water→steam) → Turbine (steam spins blades) → Generator (magnets produce electricity) → Condenser (steam→water) → Boiler. Add arrows showing energy transformation: Chemical (fuel) → Heat → Mechanical → Electrical.

Draw a triangle or staircase showing progression: Peat (muddy, brown) → Lignite (brown-black, earthy) → Bituminous (black, layered) → Anthracite (glossy black, hard). Show increasing carbon % and calorific value as you go up.
Think of two water tanks at different heights. More the height difference (Th - Tc), more the flow/energy. Efficiency = 1 - (lower height/higher height). If Th=600K, Tc=300K, η = 1-300/600 = 50%.

analogies.analogy: Fossil fuels are like a savings account of ancient sunlight. Plants captured sunlight millions of years ago through photosynthesis, and when we burn coal, oil, or gas, we're 'cashing in' that ancient stored energy.

A thermal power plant is like a restaurant: Boiler = kitchen (cooks food/steam), Turbine = waiter's tray (carries energy), Generator = cash register (converts to usable form), Condenser = dishwasher (recycles water).

analogies.analoy: Instead of using wind to grind grain, we use steam or water to spin the blades (turbine), and the spinning motion drives a generator to produce electricity - like a reverse windmill.

analogies.analogy: The condenser is like a cold glass of water on a hot day - steam hits the cold surface and turns back to water. This recycled water can be used again in the boiler, like refilling a water bottle.
